relations.note: Refines the color guidelines to restrict gold hues specifically from text applications.

quotes: GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.

rules.intent: Maintain strict digital accessibility and WCAG compliance for text readability.
rules.rule_text: Always test contrast levels to ensure compliance with WCAG AA minimum standards: at least a 4.5:1 ratio for body text, and at least 3:1 for large text (defined as >=24px, or >=19px when bold).

quotes: Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy. Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast)

rules.intent: Ensure text-to-background combinations meet visibility guidelines by listing safe defaults and prohibiting known contrast failures.
rules.rule_text: Use known-safe color pairings for text and background combinations. Approved safe combinations include: Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003D74), Dark Navy (#011E45), or Graphite (#212121) text on White (#ffffff), Light Blue (#E6F0F9), or Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) backgrounds; and White (#ffffff) text on Brand Blue (#00529b), Deep Blue (#003D74), or Dark Navy (#011E45) backgrounds. Never use Gold (#ffc60a) or Sunshine (#faa31b) text on a White (#ffffff) background.

quotes: golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Prevent low-contrast text applications of gold hues while allowing their correct usage as structural accents.
rules.rule_text: Do not use Gold (color.primary.gold = #ffc60a) or Sunshine (color.primary.sunshine = #faa31b) for standard body copy or text pairings. Gold tones are strictly reserved for structural surfaces, divider rules, and large display emphasis.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Compliance with WCAG requirements ensuring information is accessible to color-blind or visually impaired users.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely on color alone as the sole method to convey meaning, information, or interactive status. Color changes must be paired with redundant indicators, such as icons, depth, clear copywriting, or other visual feedback cues.
